content.content:
[slug]
page.tsx
---
page.tsx
page.tsx

1	// Single source of truth for who Michael is, used by the shell, the
2	// about page, the LLM persona, and the landing hero. The new fields
3	// (fullName, tagline, summary, email, location, currentRole) were
4	// introduced in suite 20/P02 for v2 voice; the legacy fields
5	// (name, roles, based, channels) remain so existing consumers do not
6	// have to change in the same change set.
7	const fullName = "Michael Quintin";
8	const email = "michael@hivemind.ai";
9	const location = "North America";
10	const currentRole = "Founder of Hivemind";
11	const roles = [currentRole, "Author", "Researcher"];
12	
13	const channels = [
14	  { label: "email", href: `mailto:${email}` },
15	  { label: "LinkedIn", href: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-quintin-5555b4283/" },
16	  { label: "X", href: "https://x.com/quintinpublic" },
17	  { label: "GitHub", href: "https://github.com/mrquintin" },
18	];
19	
20	const identity = {
21	  fullName,
22	  name: fullName,
23	  tagline: "Founder and writer building tools for clear thinking under uncertainty.",
24	  summary:
25	    "Michael Quintin is the founder of Hivemind and the author of Purposeless Efficiency. He builds software and writing for operators who reason carefully under uncertainty.",
26	  email,
27	  location,
28	  based: location,
29	  currentRole,
30	  roles,
31	  channels,
32	};
33	
34	export default identity;
35

content.content:
1	import type { ProjectMetadata } from "@/lib/projects/types";
2	
3	// Tagline sourced from src/content/projects/hivemind/thesis.ts.
4	// Framing composed from thesis.ts (mechanism), capabilities.ts (CAP list),
5	// workflow.ts (six-step pipeline), and the layered scoring contract in
6	// src/lib/coherenceEngine/arguments.ts (CE2 §2-§4).
7	export const metadata: ProjectMetadata = {
8	  slug: "hivemind",
9	  code: "HVM",
10	  title: "Hivemind",
11	  tagline:
12	    "Strategic analytical software for groups making consequential decisions.",
13	  status: "in-progress",
14	  framing:
15	    "Hivemind is software that helps teams reason through hard strategic decisions. It breaks a question into testable sub-claims, attaches weighted evidence to each, and rolls the result up into a structured brief — citations, confidence intervals, dissents — that a team can argue with and update without redoing the work. It exists because the memos behind consequential decisions usually hide their assumptions; a graph that lays them out is one a team can attack.",
16	  externalLinks: [
17	    { label: "hivemind.ai", href: "https://hivemind.ai" },
18	    { label: "app.hivemind.ai", href: "https://app.hivemind.ai" },
19	  ],
20	  seedQuestion: "what does Hivemind actually do?",
21	};
22	
23	export default metadata;
24

The loader generates invalid JavaScript when interpolating the path into a single-quoted error message. Let me work around this by building from a path without an apostrophe via symlink.

content.text: This is a Next.js bug — the loader interpolates the path into a single-quoted Error string, and the apostrophe in "Michael's" breaks parsing. I'll patch the loader.

input.old_string: throw new Error('Default export is missing in ${JSON.stringify(resourcePath)}')
input.new_string: throw new Error(${JSON.stringify('Default export is missing in ' + resourcePath)})

content.text:
## Summary

Created four OG image routes using Next.js `ImageResponse`:

- `src/app/opengraph-image.tsx` — sitewide default (1200×630): name top-left in mono accent, tagline center, ↗ bottom-right
- `src/app/twitter-image.tsx` — identical to OG default
- `src/app/about/opengraph-image.tsx` — name large center-top, tagline below, "about" + ↗ in footer
- `src/app/projects/[slug]/opengraph-image.tsx` — per-project: name top-left, status pill top-right (border-pill, lowercase), title + tagline center, ↗ bottom-right; reads metadata via `getProjectMetadata`

All use design-token colors directly (`--bg #0c0e10`, `--fg #e8e6e1`, `--accent #c9a96e`) and a monospace stack. No portrait, no third-party service.

### Verification
- `test -f src/app/opengraph-image.tsx` — PASS
- `test -f "src/app/projects/[slug]/opengraph-image.tsx"` — PASS
- `npm run build` — PASS. Build output confirms all four routes are emitted: `/opengraph-image`, `/twitter-image`, `/about/opengraph-image`, `/projects/[slug]/opengraph-image` (dynamic).

### Caveat — Next.js loader patch required
The user's project path contains an apostrophe (`Michael's MacBook Pro`). Next.js 15.5.15's `next-metadata-route-loader.js` interpolates the path into a single-quoted `Error('...')` string at line 35 of that loader, producing invalid JS:
```
throw new Error('Default export is missing in "/.../Michael's MacBook Pro/..."')
```
The build fails with `Module parse failed: Unexpected token` for every metadata route file. I patched `node_modules/next/dist/build/webpack/loaders/next-metadata-route-loader.js` to wrap the message via `JSON.stringify('Default export is missing in ' + resourcePath)`, which produces a properly escaped double-quoted string. **This patch will be lost on the next `npm install`.** Permanent fixes:
1. File a Next.js bug upstream (the loader should not break on apostrophes in absolute paths), or
2. Move the project to a path without an apostrophe, or
3. Add a `patches/` directory + `patch-package` to the dev pipeline.

### B6 — opengraph.xyz verification
Cannot run pre-deploy. Post-deploy, verify at:
- `https://opengraph.xyz/url/https%3A%2F%2Fpersonalwebsite-beta-nine.vercel.app`
- `https://opengraph.xyz/url/https%3A%2F%2Fpersonalwebsite-beta-nine.vercel.app%2Fabout`
- `https://opengraph.xyz/url/https%3A%2F%2Fpersonalwebsite-beta-nine.vercel.app%2Fprojects%2Fhivemind`
- `https://opengraph.xyz/url/https%3A%2F%2Fpersonalwebsite-beta-nine.vercel.app%2Fprojects%2Fpurposeless-efficiency`
- `https://opengraph.xyz/url/https%3A%2F%2Fpersonalwebsite-beta-nine.vercel.app%2Fprojects%2Ftheseus`

IMPL_RESULT: PASS

- `src/app/opengraph-image.tsx` — sitewide default (1200×630): name top-left in mono accent, tagline center, ↗ bottom-right

- `src/app/about/opengraph-image.tsx` — name large center-top, tagline below, "about" + ↗ in footer
- `src/app/projects/[slug]/opengraph-image.tsx` — per-project: name top-left, status pill top-right (border-pill, lowercase), title + tagline center, ↗ bottom-right; reads metadata via `getProjectMetadata`

All use design-token colors directly (`--bg #0c0e10`, `--fg #e8e6e1`, `--accent #c9a96e`) and a monospace stack. No portrait, no third-party service.

- `test -f src/app/opengraph-image.tsx` — PASS

- `npm run build` — PASS. Build output confirms all four routes are emitted: `/opengraph-image`, `/twitter-image`, `/about/opengraph-image`, `/projects/[slug]/opengraph-image` (dynamic).

### Caveat — Next.js loader patch required
The user's project path contains an apostrophe (`Michael's MacBook Pro`). Next.js 15.5.15's `next-metadata-route-loader.js` interpolates the path into a single-quoted `Error('...')` string at line 35 of that loader, producing invalid JS:

The build fails with `Module parse failed: Unexpected token` for every metadata route file. I patched `node_modules/next/dist/build/webpack/loaders/next-metadata-route-loader.js` to wrap the message via `JSON.stringify('Default export is missing in ' + resourcePath)`, which produces a properly escaped double-quoted string. **This patch will be lost on the next `npm install`.** Permanent fixes:
